questions.question: In a Router Advertisement, what does the M flag control?
options: Whether hosts use multicast for ND
Whether hosts obtain addresses via stateful DHCPv6
questions.explanation: M=1 tells hosts to request addresses from a stateful DHCPv6 server; M=0 with A=1 enables SLAAC.

questions.question: Some hosts on a dual-stack VLAN reach IPv6-only services and some do not. First place to look:
options: Compare the RA-advertised prefix against the DHCPv6 pool prefix
questions.explanation: If the RA prefix and the DHCPv6 lease prefix differ and only one is routed, hosts that picked the unrouted prefix fail intermittently.

questions.question: RFC 6724 source-address selection matters here because:
options: It picks the IPv4 address over IPv6
When a host holds addresses from two prefixes it may select the unrouted one for an IPv6 destination
It disables SLAAC when DHCPv6 is present
questions.explanation: RFC 6724 ranks candidate sources by prefix match against the destination; an unrouted prefix can be selected if it appears to match better.
